task:
Research capability-upgrade opportunities for the herdr-browser plugin at /Users/vics/dev/structupath/herdr-browser (read README.md, herdr-plugin.toml, bin/renderer.mjs, scripts/*.sh first so suggestions are grounded in what exists). Then research externally: (1) agent-browser (vercel-labs/agent-browser on GitHub) — current release version vs the tested 0.28.x; new commands/features since then that a live-viewer pane could exploit (e.g. screencast/streaming, CDP access, network logs, accessibility snapshots, trace recording, headed mode, session pool); (2) chafa — optimal flags for high-quality fast terminal rendering (symbols, dither, colors, size) and any newer canvas modes; (3) Kitty graphics protocol — best practices for animation/redraw (image IDs, placement, delete semantics, chunked transmission limits); (4) Herdr plugin API — the project is a plugin for Herdr (herdr.dev, tested against 0.7.4): what capabilities does the plugin manifest/pane protocol expose (actions, panes, link_handlers exist — what about pane input, status lines, mouse passthrough, config)? Check herdr docs/changelog for versions > 0.7.4. (5) carbonyl — current state, flags worth using for the browse pane. Deliver: a ranked list of concrete capability upgrades, each with evidence (link/command), expected user value, and rough implementation cost. Do NOT edit files — report only.

## Acceptance Contract
Acceptance level: attested
Completion is not accepted from prose alone. End with a structured acceptance report.

Criteria:
- criterion-1: Return concrete findings with file paths and severity when applicable

Required evidence: review-findings, residual-risks

Finish with a fenced JSON block tagged `acceptance-report` in this shape:
Use empty arrays when no items apply; array fields contain strings unless object entries are shown.
`criteriaSatisfied[].status` must be exactly one of: satisfied, not-satisfied, not-applicable.
`commandsRun[].result` must be exactly one of: passed, failed, not-run.
`manualNotes` and `notes` are optional strings; an empty string means no note and does not satisfy `manual-notes` evidence.
```acceptance-report
{
  "criteriaSatisfied": [
    {
      "id": "criterion-1",
      "status": "satisfied",
      "evidence": "specific proof"
    }
  ],
  "changedFiles": [
    "src/file.ts"
  ],
  "testsAddedOrUpdated": [
    "test/file.test.ts"
  ],
  "commandsRun": [
    {
      "command": "command",
      "result": "passed",
      "summary": "short result"
    }
  ],
  "validationOutput": [
    "validation output or concise summary"
  ],
  "residualRisks": [
    "none"
  ],
  "noStagedFiles": true,
  "diffSummary": "short description of the diff",
  "reviewFindings": [
    "blocker: file.ts:12 - issue found, or no blockers"
  ],
  "manualNotes": "anything else the parent should know"
}
```

criteria.must: Return concrete findings with file paths and severity when applicable
Return concrete findings with file paths and severity when applicable

criteriaSatisfied.evidence: Returned 14 ranked capability upgrades, each with concrete evidence (file paths: bin/renderer.mjs onInput/clickAt/renderImage, scripts/browse-pane.sh, herdr-plugin.toml; upstream sources: agent-browser docs/src/app/streaming/page.mdx + cli/src/native/stream/cdp_loop.rs, herdr.dev/docs/socket-api pane.graphics.* verified in local 0.7.4 schema, sw.kovidgoyal.net/kitty/graphics-protocol, chafa --help + local benchmarks, carbonyl changelog.md/usage.txt + npm dist-tags) and severity/value/cost rankings; includes one live verified bug (renderer.mjs 'i' prompt uses selector-requiring 'type' instead of 'keyboard type', reproduced against agent-browser 0.28.0)

commandsRun.command: agent-browser --version; chafa --version; carbonyl --version; herdr --version; npm view agent-browser version; npm view carbonyl version
commandsRun.summary: Installed: agent-browser 0.28.0 (latest 0.32.3), chafa 1.18.2 (=latest), carbonyl 0.0.2 (npm latest 0.0.2-next.bacf3db, next 0.0.3-next.ab80a27), herdr 0.7.4

commandsRun.command: agent-browser --help; agent-browser stream --help; agent-browser skills list
commandsRun.summary: 0.28.0 already ships stream enable/disable/status, keyboard type, mouse move/down/up/wheel, network har/requests, record, trace, snapshot, errors, get cdp-url

commandsRun.command: agent-browser --session hb-stream stream status --json + Node WebSocket client to ws://localhost:54597
commandsRun.summary: WS stream live on 0.28.0: received status + 15KB base64 JPEG frame push (vs 2.4MB chafa kitty frame)

commandsRun.command: agent-browser type 'hello' --json vs agent-browser keyboard type 'hello' --json
commandsRun.summary: type fails ('Element not found' - treats arg as selector); keyboard type succeeds — confirms live bug in renderer's 'i' prompt

commandsRun.command: chafa benchmarks: symbols/kitty formats, -O 0/5/9, -c full, --polite on/off, -w 1/9 at 100x30
commandsRun.summary: chafa kitty emits a=T,f=32 raw RGBA = 2,485,874 bytes/frame regardless of -O; ~110-140ms/frame; default piped output already 24-bit; --polite on removes chafa's ESC[?25l cursor-hide

commandsRun.command: herdr api schema --json | grep pane.graphics; GitHub API for ogulcancelik/herdr and vercel-labs/agent-browser releases
commandsRun.summary: herdr 0.7.4 has pane.graphics.set/clear/info (no stream); 0.7.5 (2026-07-21) adds pane.graphics.stream + [[startup]] hooks; agent-browser latest 0.32.3

validationOutput: chafa kitty header bytes: ESC_G a=T,f=32,s=1000,v=460,c=100,r=23,m=1,q=2 (raw RGBA, chunked)
herdr 0.7.4 schema methods include pane.graphics.set/clear/info; graphics.stream count = 0 (new in 0.7.5)
residualRisks: WebSocket stream frame format is hardcoded JPEG q80 (cdp_loop.rs) — feeding herdr pane.graphics (png/rgb/rgba only) needs a JPEG decode step unless screenshots keep supplying PNG
carbonyl is unmaintained since 2023-02 with a ~3-year-old Chromium — fine for localhost dev, a risk if the browse pane is used for untrusted sites
herdr pane.graphics.* requires [experimental].kitty_graphics = true and is experimental — API shape may change before stabilization
Upgrade #1/#2 require nontrivial renderer rewrites; sizing estimates (0.5-2 days) are rough and untested against Herdr's stream_conflict/teardown edge cases
childReport.diffSummary: No files edited — research-only task. git status shows only pre-existing .pi-subagents artifact changes from the parent session, not from this work.
reviewFindings: blocker-equivalent bug found (feature broken, not a code-change blocker): bin/renderer.mjs onInput 'i' prompt calls agent-browser 'type <text>' which requires a selector in 0.28.x and always fails; fix is one line to 'keyboard type' (upgrade #3, verified live)
no other blockers; remaining items are ranked enhancement opportunities with evidence links in the report body
childReport.manualNotes: Highest-leverage path: (1) one-line keyboard-type fix, (2) chafa --polite on / -c full, (3) carbonyl@next install line, then (4) agent-browser WebSocket stream as the viewer transport, (5) herdr pane.graphics.set/stream as the pixel layer (set works on installed 0.7.4; stream needs 0.7.5). Upgrades #1+#2 together eliminate the 4-subprocesses/sec poll, the chafa kitty 2.4MB/frame re-encode, the eval-based clicking, and the console ring-buffer reconciliation in one architecture.
